traps.description: refill() updates bucket.tokens with `tokens + added` but never caps at `capacity`. After a long idle period the bucket can hold far more than `capacity` tokens, letting a single caller burst past the intended rate limit.

Constructor does not validate `opts.capacity > 0`, `opts.refillPerSec >= 0`, or that either value is finite. Zero/negative/NaN capacity silently produces a limiter whose math is nonsense (always denies or yields NaN), instead of throwing a clear error at construction time.

The async slow path of acquire() polls `msUntilTokens` and sleeps. When `refillPerSec === 0` the bucket can never refill, but msUntilTokens returns a constant 1000 ms, so the loop sleeps forever instead of rejecting. A caller awaiting acquire() in this state hangs indefinitely.
